data.title: Apple's global iPhone revenue surges 40% month over month: Bernstein
data.description:
Apple's (AAPL) iPhone 17 sales surged in October, generating much stronger revenue than the iPhone 16 at this time last year, according to Bernstein Société Générale Group.

"Overall, iPhone 17 series delivered a 20% increase in unit sales over the iPhone 16 series based on combined sales in September and October," said Bernstein analysts, led by Mark Newman, in an investor report. "The base model of iPhone 17 contributed the largest share of this growth, up 27%, while the Pro and Pro Max models also demonstrated strong performance, both up 20%. iPhone Air sales have been underwhelming, however, trailing behind iPhone 15 Plus. Sales of iPhone Air were hurt due to lack of availability in China until late October."

The spike in sales led to Apple achieving a 24.2% market share in October, which was its highest on record. It also led to revenue gains of 14% year over year and 40% month over month.

China proved to be Apple's fastest-growing market in October as units sold increased by 38%. iPhone sales in the Americas were also stout, leading to a year-over-year revenue gain of 14.5%.

The strong iPhone 17 demand is a positive sign for companies in Apple's supply chain, such as Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) and Luxshare. Despite the recent spike in memory prices, Bernstein does not expect it to affect Apple at this point, even though the iPhone 17 has about 25% more dynamic random access memory than the iPhone 16. It is also positive for Qualcomm (QCOM) in the short term, but Bernstein expects Apple to move fully away from the company over the next few years.

data.title: Waterdrop targets long-term growth as Q3 revenue rises 38.4% and AI integration accelerates
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Waterdrop Inc. (WDH) Q3 2025

Management View

  • Xiaoying Xu, Head of Finance, reported that "overall revenue reaching over CNY 970 million, up 38.4% year-on-year." Xu highlighted that AI played a significant role in enhancing business quality and efficiency, contributing to "net profit attributable to the ordinary shareholders to about CNY 160 million, a year-on-year increase of 60.1%." Xu noted the company maintained GAAP profitability for 15 consecutive quarters and that the insurtech business delivered "operating profit reaching about CNY 180 million in this quarter, a year-on-year increase of 44.8%." Xu also pointed to the launch of Waterdrop C-Point AI, a low-code platform designed to empower employees to build and deploy AI solutions for tasks like marketing and customer service.
  • Xu stated, "In early November, we completed our fourth cash dividend distribution since IPO, with a total payout of around $10.9 million. By the end of November 2025, we had repurchased approximately 58.1 million ADS in the open market, totaling $113 million."
  • Ran Wei, Partner and GM of Insurance Technology Business & Director, detailed that the insurance business achieved "insurance-related revenue reached about CNY 870 million, increasing by 44.8% year-on-year and 17.8% quarter-on-quarter" with an operating profit margin of 20.3%. Wei emphasized the impact of AI integration on both efficiency and customer experience, stating that "the entire system is deeply integrated with our corporate data lake and AI platform, including self-training and health updates to continually refine our marketing strategy."
  • Wei noted the introduction of new insurance products, including the Quan Jia Fu Family Protection Plan and upgrades to existing offerings, as well as the rollout of AI Pro insurance model and advancements in AI-driven voice and after-sales services.
  • Jasmine Lee, Corporate Secretary, reported that Waterdrop Medical Crowdfunding had reached "approximately 485 million people had cumulatively donated a total of RMB 71.2 billion to 3.61 million patients." Lee also highlighted enhancements in risk control and transparency and said the E-Find platform "expanded collaboration network by partnering with 216 pharmaceutical and contract research organizations."

Outlook

  • Xu stated the company is "confident in exceeding full-year revenue and profit guidance." Xu added, "Moving forward, we will leverage advancing AI technologies to drive integration and innovation across our operations, fueling sustainable long-term growth."
  • The transcript did not explicitly state updated guidance figures or quantify new targets in the outlook, but the management’s language signals continued optimism for surpassing existing targets.

Financial Results

  • Xu reported "total revenue of RMB 975 million, marking a year-on-year growth of 38.4%." The insurance business generated "about RMB 870 million in revenue, up 44.8% year-on-year." Crowdfunding service fees were "around RMB 65.66 million, remaining stable year-on-year," and digital clinical trial solutions contributed "over RMB 31.85 million with a year-on-year increase of 31.3%." Operating profit was "nearly RMB 114 million, marking a substantial year-on-year increase of 329.8%." Net profit attributable to shareholders reached "RMB 158 million, up 60.1% year-on-year." The company ended the quarter with "about RMB 3.51 billion" in cash.
  • Operating costs and expenses totaled "approximately RMB 861 million, marking a year-on-year increase of 27.1%." Sales and marketing expenses were "RMB 243 million, rising 40.3% year-on-year" due to increased investment in third-party traffic channels. General and administrative expenses decreased "23.7% year-on-year to RMB 84.7 million."

Q&A

  • NOT_APPLICABLE: The provided transcript does not include any analyst questions or management responses in a Q&A format.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst sentiment cannot be determined as no analyst questions or commentary are included in the transcript.
  • Management sentiment remains positive and confident, with Xu stating, "we are confident in exceeding full-year revenue and profit guidance" and emphasizing long-term growth driven by AI integration.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone continues to reflect optimism and strong belief in AI-enabled growth, with no notable change in confidence level.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue increased from RMB 838 million in Q2 2025 to RMB 975 million in Q3 2025.
  • Insurance business revenue rose from RMB 739 million to RMB 870 million quarter-over-quarter.
  • Net profit attributable to shareholders increased from RMB 140 million in Q2 to RMB 158 million in Q3.
  • Management language in both quarters highlights the rapid advancement and integration of AI technologies but in Q3 there is a stronger emphasis on exceeding full-year guidance.
  • Strategic focus remains consistent, centered on leveraging AI to drive innovation and operational efficiency; however, Q3 highlights more advanced AI deployments and new product launches.
  • Analysts’ tone cannot be compared due to absence of Q&A in both quarters.

Risks and Concerns

  • Xu acknowledged that "discussion today will contain forward-looking statements made under the safe harbor provision" and cited risks and uncertainties as outlined in public filings.
  • No additional specific business risks or mitigation strategies were discussed in the transcript.

Final Takeaway

Waterdrop reported strong revenue and profit growth in Q3 2025, driven by further integration of AI across its insurance, crowdfunding, and healthcare businesses. Management reiterated confidence in exceeding full-year revenue and profit guidance, highlighted new product launches and expanded partnerships, and showcased substantial improvements in operational efficiency. With a healthy cash position and continued investment in AI-driven innovations, Waterdrop signaled a commitment to sustaining long-term growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Microsoft erases some losses as it denies report it cut sales quotas tied to AI
data.description:
• Microsoft (MSFT) shares erased some of its losses on Wednesday after it denied a report that it was cutting software sales quotas tied to artificial intelligence.
  • Microsoft told CNBC it has not lowered its sales quotas or targets for its salespeople. The company has not yet responded to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
  • Shares fell on Wednesday after The Information reported that the tech giant is cutting software sales quotas tied to artificial intelligence. The Windows maker has lowered its forecast for how fast customers will be able to spend money on AI agents, after a number of salespeople missed their goals, the news outlet added, citing people familiar with the matter. The quotas have been missed across multiple divisions, two salespeople in Microsoft's Azure unit told the news outlet.

(This story has been updated with Microsoft's response.)

data.title: Pharvaris achieves primary goal in late-stage trial of deucrictibant for HAE
data.description:
• Pharvaris (PHVS) is up ~18% after reporting that deucrictibant, its candidate for hereditary angioedema ("HAE") attacks, met its primary endpoint in a phase 3 study.
  • Data from the RAPIDe-3 trial showed that those on the oral bradykinin B2 receptor antagonist had a median time to onset of symptom relief in 1.28 hours, a time much faster than those on placebo.
  • Secondary endpoints, including end of progression and complete symptom resolution times were also met.
  • Pharvaris said it will begin sending applications for approval to drug regulators beginning in H1 2026.

data.title: Traders jump into iRobot and Server Robotics ahead of the White House's robotics announcement
data.description:
Highly shorted iRobot Corporation (IRBT) soared 31% in early Wednesday morning trading as the volatile swings for the consumer products company continued.

Some traders are pointing to the White House's expected announcement on support of the U.S. robotics industry as a potential catalyst for the share price jump. That connection could be debatable since iRobot (IRBT) only designs, builds, and sells consumer home robots, primarily Roomba vacuums and Braava mops, plus related smart home software and accessories worldwide, which may not qualify for government support.

Short interest on iRobot (IRBT) stands at 39% of the total float, which has added to trading volatility on the stock recently. Even accounting for the share price pop on Wednesday, iRobot (IRBT) is down more than 65% on a year-to-date basis. The Seeking Alpha Quant Rating on IRBT is flashing Strong Sell.

Autonomous restaurant delivery robot player Serve Robotics (SERV) was up 4.2% amid the sudden flurry of robotics talk.

data.title: RBC Q4 earnings top consensus on strong net interest income
data.description:
Royal Bank of Canada's (RY) fiscal Q4 2025 earnings and revenue rose past Wall Street expectations as Y/Y growth was recorded across each of its business units. What's more, the company updated its medium-term return-on-equity goal to 17% or more.

Q4 adjusted EPS of C$3.85 (US$2.76), surpassing the C$3.52 average analyst estimate, rose from C$3.84 in Q3 and C$3.07 a year earlier.

Total revenue of C$17.2B for the quarter ended Oct. 31, 2025, also beating the C$16.7B consensus, climbed from C$17.0B in the three months ended July 31, 2025, and C$15.1B in the three months ended Oct. 31, 2024.

RY shares edged up 0.3% in Wednesday morning trading in the U.S.

The lender's net interest income was C$8.65B (vs. C$8.42B Visible Alpha estimate), up from C$8.35B in Q3 and C$7.67B in Q4 2024.

Noninterest income dipped to C$8.56B in Q4 from C$8.63B in Q3, but advanced from C$7.40B in the year-earlier period.

Consolidated Q4 results include higher provisions on impaired loans, primarily in Commercial Banking, Personal Banking and Capital Markets. Provision for credit losses jumped to C$1.00B in Q4 from C$881M in the prior quarter and C$840M in Q4 2024.

Adjusted return on equity of 17.2% vs. 17.7% in Q3 and 15.1% in last year's Q4.

Preprovision pretax earnings of $30B were up 30% from a year ago, primarily reflecting higher net interest income reflecting solid average volume growth in Personal Banking and Commercial Banking and higher spreads across most of RBC's business segments.

data.title: White House to widen its AI-robotics lens; see top sector names by market cap
data.description:
The Trump administration is intensifying its focus on robotics as a part of its broader initiative to accelerate the development of artificial intelligence, Politico reported Wednesday.

Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick is actively engaging with leaders of the robotics industry and is considering an executive order to advance this sector’s growth, the report said. This push aligns with efforts to compete with China, which leads the world in industrial robotics deployment with 1.8 million robots—four times the U.S. total.

Here are the top robotic and AI-related stocks ranked by their market cap: 

  • Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD) – $350.42B
  • Analog Devices, Inc. (ADI) – $133.66B
  • Fanuc Corporation (OTCPK:FANUY) – $32.13B
  • UiPath Inc. (PATH) – $7.59B
  • ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited (ZK) – $6.84B
  • BigBear.ai Holdings, Inc. (BBAI) – $2.53B
  • Tuya Inc. (TUYA) – $1.36B
  • Radware Ltd. (RDWR) – $1.01B
  • Information Services Group, Inc. (III) – $262.88M

data.title: GitLab gets down following Q3 results; analysts point to Fed, SMB softness
data.description:
GitLab (GTLB) shares sank 15% by early market action on Wednesday following third-quarter results that once again surpassed expectations, as analysts point to a slowdown from the public sector due to the U.S. federal government shutdown and pressure among small-to-medium sized businesses.

"GTLB noted benefit driven by downstream effects of more AI-driven code generation, but headwinds from ongoing weakness in SMB (8% ARR) and lingering effects from the U.S. federal shutdown (will also impact 4Q)," said Wells Fargo analysts Ryan MacWilliams and Chris Brazeau in an investor note. "Customer adds saw pressure, with 956 net new total customers (lowest in the past 4 years). RPO and cRPO decelerated to 27% y/y and 28% y/y, respectively, which remain at elevated growth levels but leave investors questioning where the rev. guide starts next year."

Wells Fargo retained its Equal Weight rating but lowered its price target to $45 from $50.

Similarly, Mizuho maintained its Neutral rating and lowered its price target to $47 from $52.

"Broadly, while we view GTLB's platform as strategically important within DevSecOps, we continue to see ongoing risks: (1) uncertainty over whether AI will erode its developer-heavy seat model, (2) intensifying competition from GitHub and AI-native startups, (3) potentially weaker monetization than management implies, particularly for the Premium SKU, and (4) uncertain benefits from the new GTM strategy," said Mizuho analyst Gregg Moskowitz in a note.

Meanwhile, Cantor was more upbeat, reiterating its Overweight rating and $60 price target.

"GitLab Ultimate continues to drive platform adoption and success, accounting for 54% of total ARR, with seven of the firm's top ten deals in the quarter being Ultimate," said Cantor analysts Jonathan Ruykhaver and Ben Mitchell in a note. "Customers are drawn to its security and compliance features, and management highlights a payback period of less than six months and an ROI of 480% over three years."

GitLab Ultimate is the most advanced tier of the company's DevSecOps platform. It offers a comprehensive set of features for enterprises, particularly those with advanced security, compliance and planning needs.

Cantor also pointed to improved margin gains, as third quarter operating margins increased to 17.9% compared to 13.2% one year ago. Free cash flow margins also rose to 11.1%, up from 4.9% one year prior.

Finally, RBC maintained its Outperform rating and $58 price target.

"Management noted steady progress on first-order business vs. expansions, though it is not where it needs to be with room for improvement," said RBC analysts, led by Matthew Hedberg, in a Wednesday note. "They also noted Gitlab Duo Agent Platform is on schedule to go GA in the coming weeks, which is a positive. This will help transition the business model from purely seat-based to a hybrid seat/consumption model."

Competitor JFrog (FROG) was down 1%, and Microsoft (MSFT), which offers the similar GitHub had declined 2.9%.

data.title: U.S. stocks mixed after jobs data drags sentiment, Microsoft slashes AI sales quotas
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were mixed on Wednesday, after the latest private sector jobs data fell, while investors assessed Microsoft's (MSFT) software sales quotas tied to AI being cut.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last flat in late morning trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.2%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.3%.

The U.S. private sector employment fell by 32K in November, compared with the 5K consensus and October's 47K (revised from 42K), according to data released by ADP on Wednesday.

“It’s a K-shaped economy and labor market as the Nov ADP private payroll estimate indicated a 32K decline in hiring. For the past 6 out of 7 months small businesses have seen losses and were down -120K. These are firms without adequate capital depth and are likely experiencing stress in adjusting to higher prices and taxes such as tariffs,” said Joseph Brusuelas, RSM U.S. principal and chief economist.

“However, mid-size businesses added 51K and large firms added 39K as those businesses have exposure to the upper spur of the K that is doing quite well right now,” Brusuelas added.

Also, the S&P Global U.S. Composite PMI came in softer than expected at 54.2 in November, compared to October's 54.6, while the U.S. ISM Services Index edged up in November, beating consensus.

Microsoft (MSFT) shares were -2.9% after The Information reported that the tech giant is cutting software sales quotas tied to artificial intelligence.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD), on the other hand, continued its recovery, now over $92,000.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 3 basis points to 4.07%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 2 basis points to 3.50%.

On the earnings side, Salesforce (CRM) and Snowflake (SNOW), among others, will report their results.

In other economic news, the U.S. import and export prices were unchanged month-over-month in September.
